title: How to calculate standard deviation (STDEV.S vs STDEV.P)
task: Measure how spread out values are — and pick the right one of the two functions.

excel.note: S = sample (your data is a subset). STDEV.P when the range IS the entire population.

google_sheets.note: STDEV = sample, STDEVP = population; the dotted names also work.

explanation: Standard deviation is the typical distance from the mean: for 2,4,4,4,6 (mean 4) the population version gives √1.6 ≈ 1.2649. Use STDEV.S when your rows are a sample of something bigger (it divides by n−1, giving a slightly larger, unbiased estimate — here 1.4142); use STDEV.P only when the data is complete in itself. Roughly two-thirds of normal-ish data falls within one standard deviation of the mean — a quick sanity check for outliers.
